```python
# test_camel_to_snake.py
import pytest

# If camel_to_snake lives in another file, replace the next import accordingly
from your_module import camel_to_snake


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Happy-path cases
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "camel, expected_snake",
    [
        ("camelCase", "camel_case"),         # typical lowerCamel
        ("CamelCase", "camel_case"),         # typical UpperCamel / Pascal
        ("already_snake", "already_snake"),  # string that is already snake
        ("lowercase", "lowercase"),          # all lowercase, no changes
        ("Camel2Snake", "camel2_snake"),     # digits in the middle
        ("HTMLParser", "htmlparser"),        # consecutive capitals not split
    ],
)
def test_camel_to_snake_happy_path(camel, expected_snake):
    assert camel_to_snake(camel) == expected_snake


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Edge-case inputs
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "camel, expected_snake",
    [
        ("", ""),          # empty string
        ("A", "a"),        # single capital letter
        ("a", "a"),        # single lowercase letter
        ("CamelCaseX", "camel_casex"),  # capital letter at very end
    ],
)
def test_camel_to_snake_edge_cases(camel, expected_snake):
    assert camel_to_snake(camel) == expected_snake


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Error / invalid-type cases
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_input", [None, 123, 4.56, ["CamelCase"]])
def test_camel_to_snake_type_errors(bad_input):
    """
    re.sub requires a string or bytes-like object; anything else must raise
    TypeError.
    """
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        camel_to_snake(bad_input)
```
